fresh_facts.claim: Thousands of soldiers from the US Army's 82nd Airborne Division began arriving in the Middle East on March 30, 2026, including headquarters, logistics, support, and one brigade combat team.
About 2,500 Marines arrived in the Middle East over the weekend prior to March 30, 2026.

US military deployments to the Middle East include consideration of operations to seize Kharg Island (Iran's key oil export hub) or secure the Strait of Hormuz, though no decision has been made for ground incursions into Iran.
The USS Gerald R. Ford carrier group and F-22 jets were deployed to Israel in February 2026, constituting one of the largest US force postures in the region since 2003.
F-22 jets were deployed to Ovda Airbase on February 24, 2026.
Joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran occurred on February 28, 2026, beginning the 2026 Iran war.
President Trump stated on March 30, 2026 to the Financial Times that US and allied forces have struck 13,000 targets since the war began on February 28.
Trump warned on March 30, 2026 about attacking Iranian oil wells and power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not opened.
The US President warned of blowing up Iran's Kharg Island oil hub if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened, as reported on March 30, 2026.
UK Prime Minister Starmer stated on March 30, 2026 that Britain will not be dragged into war.

The Iranian regime appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, an IRGC figure, as Supreme National Security Council Secretary after Ali Larijani's killing, signaling abandonment of political balancing for full IRGC control.
Reports indicate uncertainty over Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's status and intensified power struggles following his reported killing.
Widespread blackouts and industrial infrastructure degradation from strikes are exacerbating internal economic disruption in Iran.
The Strait of Hormuz disruption from the conflict is severely impacting global oil flows.
IRGC's Tehran commander Hassan Hassanzadeh was killed, weakening capital security.
The US deployed hundreds more special forces (Rangers, Navy SEALs), raising regional troops above 50,000.
Potential US operations target the Hormuz Strait, Kharg Island, and nuclear sites in Isfahan and Natanz.
Iran's retaliatory missile and proxy attacks hit Israeli energy infrastructure, including a second Haifa refinery fire on March 30, 2026.
Strikes over the 24-hour period hit multiple Iranian cities, killing an IRGC commander and degrading military and nuclear sites.
The 2026 Iran war began on February 28, 2026 with US-Israeli strikes and is now in its fifth week as of March 30-31, 2026.
An earlier London rally on March 28, 2026 urged UK recognition of the NCRI provisional government and Maryam Rajavi's 10-point democratic plan.

regime_fall.current_status: US military presence in West Asia has reached over 57,000 troops with 82nd Airborne and special forces arriving March 29-30, 2026, as Trump maintains diplomatic pause until April 6 while threatening strikes on Kharg Island and Iranian infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed; Iran's regime simultaneously faces internal collapse with Khamenei's death, IRGC consolidation of power, and widespread infrastructure degradation from 13,000 strikes since February 28.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and allied forces have conducted 13,000 strikes since the war began on February 28, 2026, with 82nd Airborne Brigade Combat Team arriving March 30 and Trump threatening to destroy Kharg Island oil hub and Iranian power plants if Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.
